caption: Individual-soldier collision, phase 4 foundations: the engaged front-rank bodies are now PERSISTENT. Instead of re-seeding onto their formation slots every tick (phase 3), each engaged soldier springs toward its slot and integrates its own velocity -- so a soldier shoved by the cross-regiment separation pass HOLDS the displacement and eases back, rather than snapping to formation. Watch the contact line as two infantry blocks clash: the front ranks jostle, give, and recover instead of staying glued to a rigid grid, while the unengaged bulk behind still tracks formation. It stays non-authoritative -- combat and movement use the regiment circle, so outcomes are unchanged; this is the body dynamics the per-soldier combat (next slice) will resolve against. The combat math (opposed rolls, wound) also lands this PR as pure, tested functions, not yet wired in.
